glossary.plain_meaning: Environment Light lights everything using an image instead of a single direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a spotlight or point light.
glossary.mental_model: Light coming from the whole world.
glossary.example: The train yard being lit by the sky all around it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light contains settings that control the color and strength of the environment light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control drawing order.
glossary.example: Deciding what kind of sky light the train yard has.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Color tints the environment light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not replace the environment image.
glossary.example: Making the sky light warmer or cooler over the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Dimmer controls how strong the environment light is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change light direction.
glossary.example: Making the overall sky light brighter or darker.

glossary.plain_meaning: Env Map is the image used to light the scene from all directions.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the background image.
glossary.example: A picture of the sky that lights the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Env Map Rotate turns the environment image.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move objects.
glossary.example: Turning the sky image so light comes from a different side.

glossary.plain_meaning: Use Pre-Filter Maps enables prepared lighting versions of the environment image.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the original image.
glossary.example: Using a pre-calculated sky light for the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how the light is applied and drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change light color.
glossary.example: Rules for how the environment light affects the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Material controls how surfaces react to the environment light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change geometry.
glossary.example: How shiny the train looks under sky light.

glossary.plain_meaning: Draw Priority controls draw order relative to other objects.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect light strength.
glossary.example: Whether the light is processed before or after other elements.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pick Priority controls selection when items overlap.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect visuals.
glossary.example: Which object gets selected first in the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Wireframe Color sets the outline color when shown as wireframe.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect final lighting.
glossary.example: The outline color of the light helper in the yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions hold extra behavior added to the component.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for basic lighting.
glossary.example: Optional systems added to the lighting setup.

glossary.plain_meaning: Re-Init Extensions resets extension logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not reset the light itself.
glossary.example: Restarting lighting helper systems.

glossary.plain_meaning: Init Extensions on Start controls whether extensions start automatically.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect core lighting.
glossary.example: Helper systems turning on when the train yard loads.

glossary.plain_meaning: Geometry is the shape that exists in 3D space.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not light, color, or movement by itself.
glossary.example: The train cars and tracks themselves.

glossary.plain_meaning: Xform controls where the geometry is, how it is turned, and how big it is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the shape itself.
glossary.example: Placing the train on the tracks and pointing it forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Transform Order decides the order moves happen.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the move values.
glossary.example: Turning the train before moving it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate Order decides the order rotations happen.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change how much rotation happens.
glossary.example: Looking left before looking up.

glossary.plain_meaning: Translate moves the geometry.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate or resize.
glossary.example: The train moving forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate turns the geometry.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move it.
glossary.example: The train turning a corner.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scale changes size.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move or rotate.
glossary.example: The train appearing larger.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pivot is the point rotations happen around.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the geometry.
glossary.example: The axle the train turns around.

glossary.plain_meaning: Uniform Scale keeps scaling even in all directions.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control position.
glossary.example: The train growing evenly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Parent Transform Source makes geometry follow another object’s movement.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not manual movement.
glossary.example: A train car following the engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At makes geometry face a target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train always pointing toward the next station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At Up Vector defines which direction stays upright.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the target.
glossary.example: Keeping the train from flipping over.

glossary.plain_meaning: Path OP defines a path to follow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define speed.
glossary.example: The tracks the train follows.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pre-Xform applies movement before the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final position.
glossary.example: Adjusting the train before placing it on the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Apply Pre-Transform turns the pre-transform on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change main transform values.
glossary.example: Choosing whether early adjustments matter.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Transform clears transform values.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete geometry.
glossary.example: Putting the train back to default.

glossary.plain_meaning: Commit to Main Transform makes pre-transform permanent.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not add a second transform.
glossary.example: Fixing the train’s adjusted position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Instance creates many copies of the same geometry.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not unique geometry.
glossary.example: Many identical train cars made from one model.

glossary.plain_meaning: Instancing turns multiple copies on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the original shape.
glossary.example: Deciding whether to show one train car or many.

glossary.plain_meaning: Instance Count Mode decides how the number of instances is determined.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move instances.
glossary.example: How many train cars to show.

glossary.plain_meaning: Default Instance OP supplies data for instances.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not geometry.
glossary.example: A list describing where each train car goes.

glossary.plain_meaning: Texture Mode controls how textures are applied to instances.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change geometry.
glossary.example: How paint is applied to train cars.

glossary.plain_meaning: Texture Coord OP supplies texture coordinates.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the texture image.
glossary.example: Instructions for where paint goes on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: UVW are three directions used for texture placement.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not world directions.
glossary.example: Directions paint wraps around the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Custom Instance allows full custom control over instances.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not automatic instancing.
glossary.example: Placing each train car exactly where you want.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how geometry is drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change geometry shape.
glossary.example: How the train appears on screen.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Mask controls which lights affect the geometry.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the lights.
glossary.example: Only certain lights shining on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions add extra behavior logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required.
glossary.example: Extra systems added to manage the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not geometry-specific.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Handle is a control used to move or rotate other objects.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not geometry meant to be rendered as part of the scene.
glossary.example: A handle you grab to pull or turn the train cars.

glossary.plain_meaning: The Handle menu contains settings that control how the handle affects its target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define the target object itself.
glossary.example: Rules for how strongly pulling the handle moves the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Target Weight controls how much the handle influences the target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not turn the handle on or off.
glossary.example: How hard pulling the handle affects the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Twist Only Falloff limits influence to twisting motion.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not allow full movement.
glossary.example: The handle only twists the train, not moves it sideways.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotation Limits set boundaries on how far rotation is allowed.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not cause rotation by themselves.
glossary.example: The train can only turn so far before stopping.

glossary.plain_meaning: X Rotation Limits restrict rotation on the X axis.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect other axes.
glossary.example: Limiting how much the train can pitch up or down.

glossary.plain_meaning: Y Rotation Limits restrict rotation on the Y axis.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect other axes.
glossary.example: Limiting how much the train can turn side to side.

glossary.plain_meaning: Z Rotation Limits restrict rotation on the Z axis.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect other axes.
glossary.example: Limiting how much the train can roll.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how the handle is drawn in the viewer.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect how the handle controls targets.
glossary.example: How the handle looks when visible in the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Material defines the surface look of the handle when drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect control behavior.
glossary.example: The color of the handle grip.

glossary.plain_meaning: Draw Priority controls draw order relative to other items.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect interaction.
glossary.example: Whether the handle is drawn on top of the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pick Priority controls selection when things overlap.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change visuals.
glossary.example: Which handle is selected first if multiple overlap.

glossary.plain_meaning: Wireframe Color sets the outline color when shown as wireframe.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect final rendering.
glossary.example: The outline color of the handle.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Mask controls which lights affect the handle’s appearance.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect the lights themselves.
glossary.example: Which lights make the handle visible.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions add extra behavior logic to the handle.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for basic control.
glossary.example: Extra systems attached to the handle.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not handle-specific.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light creates illumination that affects objects in the scene.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create geometry or images by itself.
glossary.example: A light shining on the train yard so the trains can be seen.

glossary.plain_meaning: Xform controls where the light is, how it is turned, and its size scale.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control how bright the light is.
glossary.example: Moving a floodlight closer to the tracks and pointing it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Transform Order sets the order that movement steps happen.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change movement values.
glossary.example: Turning the light before moving it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate Order sets the order rotations are applied.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not set rotation amount.
glossary.example: Tilting the light before panning it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Translate moves the light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate the light.
glossary.example: Sliding the light along the platform.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate turns the light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the light.
glossary.example: Pointing the light toward the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scale changes the light’s transform scale.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change brightness.
glossary.example: Scaling the light rig.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pivot sets the point rotations happen around.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the light.
glossary.example: The mount the light swivels on.

glossary.plain_meaning: Uniform Scale keeps scaling equal in all directions.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect brightness.
glossary.example: Resizing the light rig evenly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Parent Transform Source makes the light follow another object’s movement.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not manual control.
glossary.example: A light mounted on a moving train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At makes the light face a target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the light.
glossary.example: The light always pointing at the lead engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At Up Vector defines which direction stays upright.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the target.
glossary.example: Preventing the light from rolling sideways.

glossary.plain_meaning: Path OP defines a path the light can follow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define brightness.
glossary.example: A light moving along the rail path.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pre-Xform applies movement before the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final position.
glossary.example: Adjusting the light before locking its position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Apply Pre-Transform turns pre-transform on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change main transform values.
glossary.example: Choosing whether early adjustments count.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Transform clears transform values.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete the light.
glossary.example: Putting the light back to default.

glossary.plain_meaning: Commit to Main Transform makes the pre-transform permanent.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not add another transform.
glossary.example: Fixing the light’s adjusted position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light settings control color, type, and falloff.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not move the light.
glossary.example: Choosing what kind of lamp lights the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Color sets the color of the light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change geometry.
glossary.example: White or warm light over the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Dimmer controls how strong the light is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change direction.
glossary.example: Turning the yard lights up or down.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Type chooses how the light behaves.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change color.
glossary.example: Spotlight versus floodlight on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Cone Angle sets how wide a spotlight spreads.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change brightness.
glossary.example: A narrow beam focused on the engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Distance Attenuated makes light fade with distance.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the light.
glossary.example: The light getting weaker farther from the lamp.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shadow controls how shadows are created by the light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change light color.
glossary.example: Shadows cast by trains on the ground.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shadow Type chooses how shadows are calculated.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move objects.
glossary.example: Hard versus soft shadows under the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shadow Resolution controls shadow detail.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change light strength.
glossary.example: Crisp shadows under wheels.

glossary.plain_meaning: View controls how the light projects into the scene.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change color.
glossary.example: How the light spreads across the yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how the light helper is drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change lighting behavior.
glossary.example: How the light icon appears in the scene.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions add extra behavior logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required.
glossary.example: Extra systems controlling the light.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not light-specific.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Camera Blend combines multiple cameras into one view.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create a camera or a scene.
glossary.example: Blending views from cameras placed along the train tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Settings control background and fog blending between cameras.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move cameras.
glossary.example: Blending sky color and fog between two train-yard views.

glossary.plain_meaning: Background Color Weight controls how much background color is blended.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change camera position.
glossary.example: How much sky color comes from one camera versus another.

glossary.plain_meaning: Background Color sets the color behind the scene.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect objects.
glossary.example: The sky behind the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog Weight controls how much fog is blended.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change fog type.
glossary.example: How much haze comes from one camera view.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog turns fog blending on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move objects.
glossary.example: Turning distance haze on in the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog Density controls how thick the fog is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change fog color.
glossary.example: Dense mist around the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog Near sets where fog starts.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect far distance.
glossary.example: Fog starting near the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog Far sets where fog reaches full strength.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect near distance.
glossary.example: Fog fully hiding distant tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog Color sets the color of the fog.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change density.
glossary.example: Gray morning fog over the yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog Alpha controls fog transparency.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move fog.
glossary.example: Light haze versus thick fog.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog Map uses an image to control where fog appears.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define fog color.
glossary.example: Fog only in low areas of the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Camera Light Mask controls which lights affect the blended camera.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the lights.
glossary.example: Only certain yard lights affecting the view.

glossary.plain_meaning: View controls how the blended camera sees space.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move cameras.
glossary.example: How wide the blended train-yard view is.

glossary.plain_meaning: Projection controls how 3D space becomes a flat image.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change camera position.
glossary.example: Wide versus flat view of the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Projection Blend controls how projection types are mixed.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the view.
glossary.example: Blending wide-angle and narrow views.

glossary.plain_meaning: Viewing Angle Method decides how viewing angles are calculated.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change camera placement.
glossary.example: How wide the camera sees the yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: FOV Angle sets how wide the view is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the camera.
glossary.example: Seeing all tracks or just one.

glossary.plain_meaning: Near sets the closest visible distance.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect far distance.
glossary.example: Objects disappearing when too close.

glossary.plain_meaning: Far sets the farthest visible distance.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect near distance.
glossary.example: Tracks fading away in the distance.

glossary.plain_meaning: IPD Shift adjusts eye separation for stereo views.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect mono view.
glossary.example: Depth perception in a stereo train view.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pre-Xform applies movement before main transforms.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final transform.
glossary.example: Adjusting the camera before locking its position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Xform controls position, rotation, and scale of the camera blend.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control blending.
glossary.example: Shifting where the blended camera sits over the yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Blend controls how multiple camera inputs are combined.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change camera settings.
glossary.example: Mixing views from cameras along the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Type sets the blending rule used.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not select cameras.
glossary.example: Crossfading between two train views.

glossary.plain_meaning: Sequence sets the order cameras are blended.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change weights.
glossary.example: Which camera comes first in the mix.

glossary.plain_meaning: Weight controls how much each camera contributes.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not turn cameras on or off.
glossary.example: One camera dominating the blended view.

glossary.plain_meaning: Mask limits where blending happens.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move cameras.
glossary.example: Blending only the center of the yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Short Rotation uses the shortest turn direction when blending.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change final orientation.
glossary.example: Avoiding long camera spins during blends.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how the camera blend helper is drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect blending behavior.
glossary.example: How the camera blend icon appears in the yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions add extra behavior logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required.
glossary.example: Extra systems controlling camera blending.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not specific to camera blending.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.

glossary.what_it_is: An FBX loads a 3D file made in another program into TouchDesigner.
glossary.example: Importing a train model that was built in Blender or Maya.

Xform.meaning: Controls where the imported model is placed and how it is oriented.

items.Pivot: The point the model rotates around.
items.Parent Transform Source: Makes the model follow another object.
items.Look At: Forces the model to face a target.
items.Path OP: A path the model can follow.

Pre-Xform.meaning: Adjustments applied before the main transform.

items.Apply Pre-Transform: Turns early adjustments on or off.

FBX.meaning: Controls how the FBX file is loaded and interpreted.

items.Import Animations: Loads animation data from the file.

Animation.meaning: Controls playback of animations inside the FBX.

Render.meaning: Controls how the FBX helper is drawn.

glossary.what_it_is: An FBX loads a 3D file made in another program into TouchDesigner.
glossary.example: Importing a train model that was built in Blender or Maya.

Xform.meaning: Controls where the imported model is placed and how it is oriented.

items.Pivot: The point the model rotates around.
items.Parent Transform Source: Makes the model follow another object.
items.Look At: Forces the model to face a target.
items.Path OP: A path the model can follow.

Pre-Xform.meaning: Adjustments applied before the main transform.

items.Apply Pre-Transform: Turns early adjustments on or off.

FBX.meaning: Controls how the FBX file is loaded and interpreted.

items.Import Animations: Loads animation data from the file.

Animation.meaning: Controls playback of animations inside the FBX.

Render.meaning: Controls how the FBX helper is drawn.

glossary.example: The train’s destination sign built as real 3D letters.

Text.meaning: What the text says and how it behaves in 3D.

items.Word Wrap: Moves words to new lines automatically.
items.Width Affected by FOV or Ortho Width: Links text width to camera view.
items.Face Camera: Always turns text toward the camera.
items.Depth Scale S-Curve: How text depth changes with distance.
items.Lift Toward Camera: Pulls text closer to the camera.

items.Apply Pre-Transform: Turns early movement on or off.

Instance.meaning: Creates many copies of the same text.

Instance 2.meaning: Extra instance controls for orientation and texture.

Render.meaning: How the text geometry is drawn.

Extensions.meaning: Extra logic added to the component.

items.Node View: How it appears in the network.

glossary.what_it_is: A Null is an empty placeholder that holds position and connections.
glossary.example: An invisible point in the train yard used to line things up.

Xform.meaning: Controls where the Null exists in space.

items.Parent Transform Source: Makes the Null follow another object.
items.Look At: Forces the Null to face a target.
items.Path OP: A path the Null can follow.

FreeXform.meaning: Allows interactive movement in the viewer.

items.Enable: Turns free transform controls on or off.

Render.meaning: Controls how the Null helper is drawn.

items.Render: Turns helper drawing on or off.

Extensions.meaning: Extra logic added to the component.

items.Node View: How it appears in the network.

glossary.what_it_is: USD loads and plays Universal Scene Description files.
glossary.mental_model: A scene container that brings in a whole world.
glossary.example: Importing an entire train yard scene built somewhere else.

Xform.meaning: Controls where the USD scene is placed and how it moves.

items.Parent Transform Source: Makes the scene follow another object.
items.Path OP: A path the scene can follow.

Pre-Xform.meaning: Early movement applied before the main transform.

items.Apply Pre-Transform: Turns early movement on or off.

Play.meaning: Controls animation playback inside the USD.

USD.meaning: Controls how the USD file is imported.

items.Direct to GPU: Uploads data straight to the GPU.

Render.meaning: Controls how the USD helper is drawn.

Extensions.meaning: Extra logic added to the component.

items.Node View: How it appears in the network.

glossary.what_it_is: A UI component that can be clicked or pressed.
glossary.mental_model: A physical button on a control panel.
glossary.example: A button that starts the train moving.

Drag and Drop.meaning: Controls what happens when this button is dragged or receives drops.

items.When Dragging This: What the button does while being dragged.
items.On Dropping Into: What happens when something is dropped onto the button.

Children.meaning: Controls how child UI elements are arranged inside the button.

items.Max Per Line: Maximum items per row or column.

Layout.meaning: Controls where the button sits and how big it is.

glossary.what_it_is: A UI box that holds other UI elements.
glossary.example: A panel that holds buttons and sliders for the train controls.

Drag and Drop.meaning: Controls how this container behaves when dragging or receiving items.

items.When Dragging This: What happens while the container is dragged.
items.On Dropping Into: What happens when something is dropped inside.

Children.meaning: Controls how child UI elements are arranged inside the container.

items.Max Per Line: Maximum items per row or column.

Layout.meaning: Controls where the container sits and how big it is.

what_it_is: A UI element used to enter or display text or numbers.
mental_model: A text box or number box.
example: Typing the train’s speed into a control panel.

Field.meaning: Controls what kind of input the field accepts and how it behaves.

items.Field Type: What kind of value the field holds (text, number, etc.).
items.Enable Field Format Numbers: Turns number formatting rules on or off.
items.Field Decimal Digits: How many digits appear after the decimal.
items.Field Trailing Zeros: Keeps zeros at the end of numbers.
items.Field Leading Zeros: Adds zeros to the front of numbers.
items.Render Password Protected: Hides characters as they are typed.
items.Enter as New Line with No Shift: Pressing Enter adds a new line.
items.Render Cursor: Shows or hides the text cursor.
items.Focus Order: Order this field receives focus when tabbing.

what_it_is: A UI component that stores shader values and code.
mental_model: A control box for GPU math and shaders.
example: Sending color, numbers, and images into a shader that draws the train.

Constant.meaning: Defines single named values sent to the shader.

items.Constant: Turns a constant on or off.
items.Constant Name: The name used inside the shader.
items.Value: The number sent to the shader.

Vectors.meaning: Defines multi-number values sent to the shader.

items.Vector Name: The name used inside the shader.
items.Value: A group of numbers sent together.

Color.meaning: Defines color values sent to the shader.

items.Color Name: The name used inside the shader.
items.RGBA: Red, green, blue, and alpha values.

items.Sampler: Turns the sampler on or off.
items.Name: The name used inside the shader.
items.TOP: The image input sent to the shader.

items.Vertex Shader: Code that controls position and shape.
items.Pixel Shader: Code that controls color and pixels.

what_it_is: A UI component that displays data in rows and columns.
example: A list showing train schedules with rows for times and columns for stations.

List.meaning: Controls how the list displays and behaves.

items.Callbacks: Hooks for reacting to user actions.
items.Lock First Row: Keeps the top row fixed when scrolling.
items.Lock First Column: Keeps the first column fixed when scrolling.

what_it_is: A UI component that displays the output of another operator.
mental_model: A window that shows what another node is producing.
example: A screen showing the live video feed of the train camera.

OP Viewer.meaning: Controls how another operator is displayed and interacted with.

items.Operator Viewer Interactive: Allows direct interaction with the viewed operator.
items.Center Scale: Centers and scales the content inside the viewer.
items.Draw TOP Directly: Draws the image output without extra processing.
items.Use Content Size: Matches the viewer size to the content size.

what_it_is: A UI component that displays the output of another operator.
mental_model: A window that shows what another node is producing.
example: A screen showing the live video feed of the train camera.

OP Viewer.meaning: Controls how another operator is displayed and interacted with.

items.Operator Viewer Interactive: Allows direct interaction with the viewed operator.
items.Center Scale: Centers and scales the content inside the viewer.
items.Draw TOP Directly: Draws the image output without extra processing.
items.Use Content Size: Matches the viewer size to the content size.

what_it_is: A UI component that shows or follows another selected operator.
mental_model: A window that tracks whatever you point at.
example: A panel that always shows the currently selected train component.

Select.meaning: Controls how selection is followed and displayed.

items.Select Panel: Chooses which panel or operator is being followed.
items.Match Size: Resizes to match the selected item.
items.Follow Selection: Automatically updates when the selection changes.

what_it_is: A UI control for changing a value by dragging.
example: Sliding a control to change the train’s speed.

Slider.meaning: Controls how the slider behaves and what value it outputs.

items.Label: Text shown next to or on the slider.
items.Value: The current value of the slider.

what_it_is: A UI component that displays tabular data.
example: A table showing train times by row and station by column.

Table.meaning: Controls what table data is shown and how it is displayed.

items.Table Attribute Stat: Shows statistics for the whole table.
items.Values Stat: Shows statistics for the cell values themselves.

Table Align.what_it_is: How text is aligned inside table cells.

meaning.Left: Text starts at the left edge of the cell.
meaning.Center: Text is centered in the cell.
meaning.Right: Text ends at the right edge of the cell.

meaning.Pixels: Font size is fixed in screen pixels.
meaning.Relative: Font size scales with the component size.

Table Offset.what_it_is: Shifts which part of the table is visible.

Fixed Aspect.Off: Width and height can change independently. The widget can stretch.

Horizontal Mode.Fixed Width: Width stays constant, position changes around it.
Horizontal Mode.Fill: Width expands to fill available horizontal space.
Horizontal Mode.Anchors: Width is determined by left/right anchor positions.

Vertical Mode.Fixed Height: Height stays constant, position changes around it.
Vertical Mode.Fill: Height expands to fill available vertical space.
Vertical Mode.Anchors: Height is determined by bottom/top anchor positions.

Parent Alignment.Allow: Parent alignment rules are applied to this widget.
Parent Alignment.Ignore: Parent alignment rules are not applied.
Parent Alignment.Ignore Margins: Parent alignment applies, but margins are skipped.

what_it_is: A container for building your own custom component.
mental_model: An empty box you build systems inside.
example: A custom control box that holds logic for how the train system works.

Base.meaning: The Base operator has no operator-specific parameters.

what_it_is: A component that runs a separate process to execute logic or playback independently.
mental_model: A motor that runs on its own timeline.
example: A train engine that keeps running even if the control room pauses.

Engine.meaning: Controls what the engine loads and how it recovers.

items.Cogs File: The file that defines what the engine runs.
items.Reload on Crash: Automatically reloads if the engine crashes.
items.Asset Paths: File paths the engine can access.
items.Callback DATs: DATs that receive engine event callbacks.

Tune.meaning: Controls timing, synchronization, and playback behavior.

items.Synchronized: Engine timing stays locked to the main system.
items.Independent: Engine runs on its own timing.

Advanced.meaning: Controls engine creation and process behavior.

items.On Engine Comp Create: Callback when the engine component is created.

what_it_is: A component that runs a separate process to execute logic or playback independently.
mental_model: A motor that runs on its own timeline.
example: A train engine that keeps running even if the control room pauses.

Engine.meaning: Controls what the engine loads and how it recovers.

items.Cogs File: The file that defines what the engine runs.
items.Reload on Crash: Automatically reloads if the engine crashes.
items.Asset Paths: File paths the engine can access.
items.Callback DATs: DATs that receive engine event callbacks.

Tune.meaning: Controls timing, synchronization, and playback behavior.

items.Synchronized: Engine timing stays locked to the main system.
items.Independent: Engine runs on its own timing.

Advanced.meaning: Controls engine creation and process behavior.

items.On Engine Comp Create: Callback when the engine component is created.

Animation.meaning: Controls how animation time is interpreted and played.

items.Animation Time Reference: Which time source the animation follows.
items.Channels Data: Where animation values are read from.
items.Play Mode: How animation plays (forwards, backwards, looping, etc.).
items.Cue: Named or indexed points used to jump in time.
items.Cue Point: The exact time or frame a cue refers to.
items.Input Index Unit: What unit the input index uses (frames, seconds, etc.).
items.Cyclic Range: Whether the animation wraps around when it reaches the end.
items.Specify Edit Attributes: Chooses which animation attributes can be edited.
items.Edit Animation: Enables direct editing of animation data.

Range.meaning: Defines the portion of time the animation is allowed to use.

Type.Timeline: Uses the global or parent timeline.
Type.Custom: Uses a manually defined time range.

items.Start: The starting point of the animation range.
items.Trim Left: Cuts time from the beginning of the range.
items.Trim Right: Cuts time from the end of the range.

what_it_is: A component that plays back animation data using time and channels.
mental_model: A timeline player that reads animation values.
example: Playing the train’s movement and rotation over time.

Animation.meaning: Controls how animation data is interpreted and played.

items.Animation Time Reference: Which time source the animation follows.
items.Channels DAT: Where the animation values come from.
items.Lock the Timeline: Forces animation to stay locked to the timeline.
items.Use Input Index: Uses an external index instead of time.
items.Output Full Range: Outputs values across the full animation range.
items.Queue Point: A specific queued position in the animation.
items.Input Index Units: Units used for the input index (frames, seconds, etc.).
items.Cyclic Range: Wraps animation when reaching the end.
items.Specify Edit Attributes: Chooses which attributes can be edited.

Range.meaning: Defines which portion of the animation is active.

items.Start: The starting point of the animation range.
items.End: The ending point of the animation range.
